title: Ram Temple donation row: Champat Rai, Anil Mishra resign
content: Allegations of corruption was raised against Mr. Rai with regard to donations received by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra (SRJTK) Trust.

title: KTR asks cadre to step up drive for membership, voter enrolment under SIR
content: Addressing a meeting of key BRS workers from Maheshwaram constituency in Rangareddy district, KTR said the State was witnessing ‘only destruction and tragedy’, and accused the ruling Congress of indulging in huge scams.

title: Artist from Karnataka’s Puttur wins VB-G RAM G logo design contest
content: ‘The selected logo captures the essence of the Act by symbolising human dignity, collective effort, and the transformative journey towards Viksit Bharat,’ a note from the Union Ministry of Rural Development said.

title: Bhagyaraj’s genius effortlessly melded comedy and crisis
content: The fact that Bhagyaraj brought down the hero from a pedestal – a bespectacled, skinny youngster not afraid of being vulnerable, poor, or self-deprecatory, and willing to be laughed at – lent itself to the humour track

title: Trump likely to visit India in 2027: Rubio
content: “I look forward to returning myself before the end of the year and setting up a Presidential visit in the early parts of next year,” Mr. Rubio said

title: K. Bhagyaraj: the quintessential man-next-door hero of Tamil cinema
content: As a bespectacled hero, Bhagyaraj cut through the preconceived notions of how a celluloid figure should appear

title: AGICL invites global bids for Amaravati riverfront master plan ahead of Krishna Pushkaralu-2028
content: The 27-km riverfront, from Undavalli to Vykuntapuram, includes seven islands, with a pilot stretch at Uddandirayunipalem to be ready before the 2028 Pushkaralu

title: Vertical development of Bengaluru inevitable: CM D.K. Shivakumar
content: The Chief Minister also announced that Kempe Gowda Jayanti would no longer remain confined to Vidhana Soudha but would be celebrated every year in one of Bengaluru’s 28 Assembly constituencies over the next 28 years.

title: Politics: where the versatile Bhagyaraj could not make a dent
content: Actor-director K. Bhagyaraj passed away following a heart attack on Saturday; known for his versatility in the film world, Mr. Bhagyaraj, however, had a political stint that did not take off

title: What is the 'deal'; is CM Mann buying peace with BJP: Punjab Congress to AAP Government on VB-G RAM G
content: "What is the deal?"  Amrinder Singh Raja said and stressed that the timing of implementing the scheme, which was notified on June 26 by the Punjab Government, is as important as it is surprising

title: Ram temple ‘fraud’: SIT finds blatant SOP violations
content: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) like deploying a security guard during a counting process, frisking of personnel while entering and leaving the counting room, and preserving CCTV footage of the donation-counting process for 180 days were violated

title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT questions Unnikrishnan Potti ahead of submission of status report in Kerala HC
content: Potti’s questioning last week assumes legal and political significance, as the SIT is scheduled to submit its latest report in the case to the High Court on Monday (June 29), even as the highly undercover investigation continues to rivet public attention and remains an animating factor in Kerala politics.

title: Former Kolkata councillor Tarakeswar Chakraborty arrested on cheating, forgery charges
content: Tarakeswar Chakraborty, a former TMC councillor of ward 104, was arrested around midnight after two FIRs were registered against him at the Survey Park police station, the police said.

title: PM Modi arrives in Seychelles on three-day official visit
content: The Prime Minister, who is undertaking the state visit at the invitation of President Herminie, will participate in the National Day celebrations as the Guest of Honour

title: ‘Great loss to Indian cinema’: Condolences pour in for actor-director Bhagyaraj
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay said the veteran filmmaker and actor will be laid to rest with full State honours

title: Minister for Endowments Anam visits Srisailam temple, says focus on development of temples
content: On a visit to the temple, the Endowments Minister sought better queue management and amenities, and reviewed 297 staff houses built at ₹60 crore at Sunnipenta

title: Bhagyaraj passes away | Live updates: TN to accord state honours, says CM Vijay
content: He began his film career as an assistant director to veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja on the 1977 film 16 Vayathinile.

title: India's Gaza policy balanced, Congress doing vote bank politics: BJP
content: Sonia Gandhi, through her article, is trying to misinform people and conceal the real truth, says BJP leader Shehzad Poonawalla

title: KTR showcased his ignorance at Parigi meeting: MLA Ram Mohan Reddy
content: Addressing a press conference at the Congress Legislature Party office, he said KTR’s meeting failed to attract even a modest gathering

title: ED plea in Kerala HC seeks cancellation of bail for accused in attack on its officials
content: The ED moves the High Court seeking revocation of the bail granted to Harish Kumar, the ninth suspect in the attack on ED officials during a raid on former Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan’s residence in May, by the Thiruvananthapuram Sessions Court on June 24.

title: AMMA crisis: Kadavanthra police say no ground to book Tini Tom based on Ansiba’s complaint
content: In a report submitted to the Assistant Commissioner of Police, Ernakulam Central, the station house officer of Kadavanthra stated that the investigators could not find solid grounds to register an FIR

title: Koppal highway accident: Five killed, five injured in head-on collision
content: All the victims were residents of Kabbar village in Rattihalli taluk of Haveri district and were travelling in an Omni van to Mantralayam via Koppal when the accident occurred.

title: Explained | What is the Ram Temple embezzlement case
content: The FIR alleges a coordinated theft operation involving these temple personnel and cash-counting staff systematically pilfered cash from donation boxes and hid temple valuables during the counting and sorting phases

title: Agriculture department urges coastal Karnataka farmers to subscribe to crop insurance scheme
content: If sowing fails in 75% of a notified area due to deficient rains, insured farmers will get 50% of the sum insured straightaway

title: Pulse Polio drive from June 28; 2.83 lakh children to get drops in Anantapur district
content: Drops will be given to children under five at 1,623 booths across the district from June 28 to July 1, with door-to-door visits on June 29 and 30

title: Radio-collared elephant found dead in Kerala forest
content: A team that was tracking the movement of the elephant found the pachyderm lying motionless in the forest.

title: Karnaprayag clash: Arrested Nihang Sikhs may move court for bail
content: According to the sources, if the court grants them bail, the administration may allow them to return to their home State of Punjab

title: K. Bhagyaraj: Tamil cinema’s master of screenplay and storytelling
content: The success formula of Bhagyaraj’s early hit movies was fundamentally simple. His films were structured around personal or situational conflicts arising in the lives of ordinary individuals, a screenplay moving toward the resolution of that conflict, good songs, and plenty of situational comedy sprinkled throughout

title: Trash, toxins and sewage: The state of Dakshina Pinakini
content: A seasonal river once, Dakshina Pinakini flows today perennially due to the continuous inflow of treated, untreated and partially treated wastewater

title: Self-financing medical, dental colleges told to collect fee only for 4.5 years
content: The circular by the Directorate of Medical Education and Research reiterated the National Medical Commission’s public notice, which directed all medical colleges/institutions/universities to collect MBBS course fee only for the prescribed academic duration of 4.5 years

title: Athirappilly charts biodiversity-led path to sustainable development
content: People’s Biodiversity Register documents over 1,800 species of flora and fauna as panchayat seeks to link conservation with community livelihoods through Access and Benefit Sharing

title: Why it took nearly a year for Operation Sindoor martyrs’ names to reach the National War Memorial
content: Every name engraved on the Tyag Chakra undergoes multiple levels of verification to ensure complete accuracy before becoming part of the country’s official military history

title: Warehouse collapse: Mazdoor Cell seeks FIR against former mayor Firhad Hakim, two ex-councillors
content: The complaint was submitted by the BJMC, South Kolkata district, at Taratala police station on June 25 in the wake of the warehouse collapse on Transport Depot Road in Taratala that claimed at least 16 lives

title: Student ID cards will not carry caste details, clarifies Tamil Nadu Minister Sengottaiyan
content: Minister for Revenue and Disaster Management K.A. Sengottaiyan had said the State government would issue identity cards containing details such as students’ address, community certificate, blood group, Aadhaar number, and phone number to Class X and Class XII students in the first phase

title: Pune fort murder: Police summon Siya Goyal's parents, brother for questioning
content: Siya's parents — Pravin and Puja Goyal — and brother Sahil arrived at the police station in the morning

title: YSRCP leaders’ visit to Amaravati villages blocked by farmers, scuffle breaks out
content: The leaders had gone under the CRDA Rythu Parirakshana Committee banner; farmers said they had no right to visit, and a constable was reportedly injured in stone-throwing

title: Andhra Pradesh Pulse Polio drive from June 28 to cover 49.2 lakh children
content: Drops will be given at 29,873 booths on June 28, with house-to-house visits on June 29 and 30 to cover children missed at the booths

title: Ashok Gehlot questions ‘elusive’ justice in Kanhaiya Lal murder case, says BJP used issue for elections
content: The Congress leader stated that despite the NIA investigating and a special court hearing the case, there has been no significant progress under BJP governments at the Centre and State

title: KMC Commissioner expresses ire over supervision teams of Anna Canteens
content: On a surprise visit, the Commissioner found three of the seven staff present and warned that he would complain to the Anna Canteen CEO if it continued

title: Patna court extends Khan Sir's protection from arrest till June 30 in coaching firing case
content: The case relates to a firing incident that occurred in early June after Khan Sir's coaching institute was allegedly vandalised by a group of miscreants. His security guards are accused of opening fire during the incident.

title: DGP Harish Kumar Gupta reviews Chittoor policing, calls for stronger tech-driven measures
content: The Director General of Police praised the district’s drone-assisted and CCTV-backed policing, and asked officers to keep it transparent and responsive

title: Sonia Gandhi hits out at Modi government’s ‘stony silence’ on Gaza ‘genocide’
content: Amidst the growing public backlash against Israel and the international community’s cognisance of the unjustifiable brutality unleashed on Gaza, India remains a lone voice of silence, says Congress leader Sonia Gandhi

title: Over 150 Nihangs camping at Paonta Sahib gurdwara, say won’t leave until arrested members released
content: On June 16, a dispute in a Uttarakhand market between residents and Nihangs escalated into violence, injuring several locals and one Nihang. Police later arrested four Nihangs and registered a case

title: Vijayawada division loaded 40.64 million tonnes of freight in 2025-26, earned ₹4,136 crore
content: The Divisional Railway Manager asked customers for new logistics ideas and sought CCTVs at all goods sheds, citing ₹4,136 crore in freight earnings last year

title: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann’s fake video controversy | Explained
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann has been embroiled in a controversy over a fake video. The Hindu explains the reason for the controversy, a new twist, the Chief Minister’s defence, and the road ahead for the AAP ahead of the 2027 Assembly election

title: Nearly 6 crore names have been deleted in first yearof SIR of electoral rolls
content: Post Bihar SIR, its voters' list was pruned by nearly 65 lakh amid allegations by the Opposition and activists that the poll authority was working at the behest of the BJP to disenfranchise citizens for want of documents

title: K. Bhagyaraj, prominent actor and director, passes away
content: Hospital sources told The Hindu that he suffered a cardiopulmonary arrest and could not be resuscitated

title: Why is India’s humanitarian aid in response to Venezuelan earthquake called Operation Amistad?
content: The name is derived from the Spanish word amistad, meaning friendship, reflecting India’s solidarity with the South American nation in a time of crisis

title: 'Committed to ensuring fair probe': Ram Mandir Trust confirms Champat Rai's resignation
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has received resignations from General Secretary Champat Rai and trustee Anil Mishra amidst allegations of donation embezzlement at the Ayodhya Ram Mandir. The trust assures devotees their offerings are safe and accounted for, while confirming an FIR has been registered following an SIT probe. Legal proceedings are underway to address the controversy and restore public confidence.

title: 'Sooryavanshi is breathing down his neck': Samson gets blunt warning
content: Following India's T20I loss to Ireland, former player Sanjay Manjrekar warns Sanju Samson about the rising competition.  With young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the horizon, Samson faces pressure to perform.  While Sooryavanshi's talent is acknowledged, Manjrekar suggests he might not have single-handedly altered the game's outcome, emphasizing the difficulty of the chase and the current lack of openings at the top of the order.

title: 24 hours before exam, Maharashtra TET postponed after suspected paper leak in Thane
content: The Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) 2026, slated for June 28, has been postponed due to a suspected question paper leak. Bhiwandi police apprehended individuals with examination-related materials, which MSCE officials confirmed matched the TET paper. This breach of integrity prompted the council to reschedule the exam, with a new date to be announced soon. Candidates are urged to await official communication.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'Do not need validation': PoK 'PM' mocks Pak defence minister over 'not proper Kashmiris' remark
content: The "Prime Minister" of Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, Faizal Mumtaz Rathore, has strongly criticized Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif for questioning the Kashmiri identity of residents in Rawalkot and Mirpur. Rathore accused Asif of creating divisions and urged him to apologize for his remarks, rather than blaming local governance. This dispute unfolds amidst widespread protests in PoK over inflation and upcoming elections, where a joint committee has demanded significant reforms.

title: Romelu Lukaku becomes Belgium's highest FIFA WC scorer with record-breaking goal
content: Lukaku reached the milestone during Belgium's comfortable win, which helped them finish at the top of Group G.

title: Vaiko's MDMK exits DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance after nine years
content: The Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (MDMK) has exited the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance after a nine-year partnership. The party cited ideological differences and a perceived weakening of its identity within the alliance as reasons for its departure. MDMK leaders expressed dissatisfaction with their treatment, including contesting elections under the DMK's symbol. The move follows a period of significant political shifts in Tamil Nadu after the recent Assembly elections.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: Couple denied boarding despite valid passes, spent night at airport; airline to pay Rs 62,000
content: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: K. Bhagyaraj's net worth: Inside the Rs 70 crore legacy of Tamil cinema's 'King of Screenplay'
content: Tamil cinema's 'King of Screenplay', K. Bhagyaraj, leaves behind an estimated Rs 50-70 crore legacy, including a Rs 10 crore Chennai property. Renowned for his unique focus on family narratives and witty screenplays, he directed and acted in numerous classics over five decades. Beyond his financial success, Bhagyaraj's true enduring asset is his profound influence on storytelling in the industry, inspiring generations of filmmakers.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: Operation Tiger 3.0 on, 14 UBT MLAs will defect, claims Shiv Sena mantri
content: Shiv Sena minister Gulabrao Patil claimed 'Operation Tiger 3.0' is underway, with 14 Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs set to join Eknath Shinde's faction. However, another minister, Uday Samant, downplayed the operation but admitted MVA MLAs are interested in joining Shiv Sena. Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray is touring constituencies of recently defected MPs, with his party's Vidarbha MLAs pledging support for his upcoming rally.

title: Eye on Pakistan & China: Anti-submarine Eurodrone spurs India's layered maritime defence push
content: The development of a new anti-submarine warfare drone, a joint venture between Japan and Europe, highlights the growing importance of unmanned platforms in maritime security. This development is particularly relevant for India, facing expanding submarine fleets from Pakistan and China. The article emphasizes persistent surveillance, manned-unmanned teaming with existing assets like P-8I aircraft, and cost-effective coverage as key takeaways for strengthening India's naval defence capabilities.

title: Forget Temple Run, Delhi footpaths turn into real-life obstacle course for pedestrians
content: Delhi's footpaths, meant for safe pedestrian movement, are largely unusable. Reporters found markets, hospital zones, office hubs, and educational areas where pavements are blocked by shops, parked vehicles, vendors, and construction. This forces pedestrians onto busy roads, making walking a dangerous and difficult ordeal, despite the Supreme Court recognizing it as a fundamental right.

title: Lashkar-e-Taiba leaders attend funeral of Shoaib Akhtar's brother in Islamabad: Report
content: Senior Lashkar-e-Taiba leaders, including deputy chief Saifullah Kasuri, were reportedly seen at the funeral of former cricketer Shoaib Akhtar's brother in Islamabad. The presence of these banned terror group members, alongside the head of LeT's political wing, raises concerns about their continued influence and operational freedom in Pakistan. Kasuri has a history of anti-India rhetoric and threats.

title: Red Fort car blast case: NIA files chargesheets against 3 more accused
content: The National Investigation Agency has filed a supplementary chargesheet against three more individuals, including an absconding suspect, in the 2025 Red Fort car bomb blast case. This brings the total accused to 13. The explosion tragically claimed 11 lives, prompting a significant counter-terror investigation. The NIA's latest filing is part of its ongoing efforts to unravel the conspiracy behind the deadly incident.

title: Ladakh struggled with water for years, then Sonam Wangchuk created ice stupa as reservoir
content: In Ladakh's arid Himalayan landscape, a revolutionary solution to water scarcity has emerged. Engineer Sonam Wangchuk pioneered the 'ice stupa,' an artificial glacier built in a conical shape. These towers store winter meltwater, releasing it gradually during the crucial spring planting season. This low-cost, energy-free innovation ensures vital irrigation for agriculture, transforming a challenging environment and offering hope to communities worldwide facing similar water crises.

title: 'He said he'd hit 13 sixes': Sangakkara shares stunning Vaibhav Sooryavanshi story
content: Teenage batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is making waves, even before his India debut. Rajasthan Royals coach Kumar Sangakkara shared anecdotes highlighting the 15-year-old's remarkable confidence and fearless approach. Sangakkara recounted how Sooryavanshi predicted a chase victory and fearlessly took on Jofra Archer in nets, leaving seasoned players stunned by his audacious talent.

title: Sikh leaders allege third-degree treatment, turbans removed from pilgrims in Uttarakhand
content: Sikh leaders from Haryana have voiced strong concerns over a recent clash involving pilgrims in Uttarakhand, alleging "third-degree torture" of four arrested community members. They demand immediate release of the pilgrims and registration of cases against locals involved. Discussions have been held with Haryana and Uttarakhand chief ministers, who have reportedly given assurances.

title: Ram temple donation row: VHP VP Champat Rai’s personal driver among 8 arrested for theft
content: Eight individuals are named in the Ram temple cash embezzlement controversy, each with specific roles. While personal driver Ram Shankar Yadav alias Tinnu oversaw operations, a retired banker, Subhash Srivastava, supervised cash counting. Six others from a private agency, including counting agents like Anukalp Mishra and Lavkush Mishra, were involved in counting and transporting donations, with some relatives of accused also implicated in alleged anomalies and fraudulent receipt generation.

title: Debt, revenge: Telangana man watched 'how-to-kill' videos, then murdered 4 relatives
content: A chilling family murder in Nalgonda, Telangana, has been solved with the arrest of four individuals, including the prime accused, a relative driven by debt and revenge. Police revealed the accused meticulously planned the killings after watching online videos, motivated by robbery and a desire to silence witnesses. Stolen gold and documents were later pawned.

title: NCERT denies dropping Preamble from Class 9 textbook
content: NCERT has clarified that the Preamble remains in its Class 9 Social Science textbooks, refuting removal claims. Sources indicate a curriculum redesign under the National Curriculum Framework, with topics redistributed. Constitutional values are introduced earlier and will be detailed in Grade 10. The revised textbook, however, introduces a chapter on the 1975-77 Emergency, a significant addition for students.

title: Just 14% of MSMEs get formal loans despite India's digital finance boom: Report
content: Despite India's advanced digital payments, millions of small businesses struggle for formal finance, with only 14% accessing institutional credit. A significant credit gap, potentially exceeding Rs 50 lakh crore, hinders economic growth. While financial account ownership is high, formal credit access and insurance penetration lag global averages, highlighting critical inclusion gaps requiring policy reforms for broader economic participation and resilience.

title: 'Cricket link' surfaces in Pune murder case; Siya's parents deny knowing affair with Chetan
content: An investigation into businessman Ketan Agarwal's death has uncovered new details about prime accused Siya Goyal's alleged relationship with Chetan Chaudhary. Police suspect Siya's brother, Sahil Goyal, knew about the affair, while Siya's father denies any prior knowledge of her relationship or reluctance towards her engagement to Ketan. Both Siya and Chetan are in police custody as the probe continues.

title: Delhi YouTuber Gurman Singh 'Road Safety Wala' arrested for targeting women riders
content: A 32-year-old YouTuber, known as "Road Safety Wala," has been arrested for deliberately crashing into women riders and minor girls' vehicles, then posting the disturbing videos online for views and revenue. The accused confessed to targeting women for greater engagement and personal gratification. Police recovered a mobile phone and are investigating further into his social media activities.

title: Neeraj Chopra to compete at Asian Games 2026, AFI confirms
content: The two-time Olympic medallist Neeraj Chopra has already confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games, and the AFI has now made it clear that he will also be part of India's campaign at the Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya from September 19 to October 4.

title: Russia strikes Ukraine's Naftogaz production facilities with ballistic missiles, drones
content: In a recent onslaught, Ukraine's energy infrastructure was heavily targeted by Russian forces, leading to significant damage in the Poltava and Kharkiv regions over two days. The Naftogaz Group confirmed that despite the impact on critical energy facilities and a shuttle bus, all employees remained unharmed due to comprehensive safety measures. Damage assessments are ongoing to evaluate the full extent of the destruction.

title: CM Vijay announces state honors for K Bhagyaraj; funeral time and date revealed
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin announced state honors for the final journey of veteran filmmaker K. Bhagyaraj, who passed away, leaving the film industry in mourning. Bhagyaraj, lauded as the 'King of Screenplay,' was celebrated for his multi-talented contributions as a director, actor, and writer. His films, noted for their human values and social messages, will continue to inspire.

title: Housing sales jump 19%, new supply soars 43% across top 9 cities despite global uncertainty: Report
content: India's real estate sector is experiencing impressive momentum, marked by a 19% increase in sales during the April-June quarter, amounting to over 1.12 lakh units in nine major cities. New construction supply has also jumped by 43%. Bengaluru is at the forefront, showcasing excellent sales results, while southern regions and Mumbai reflect solid demand. The sector's strength lies in unwavering economic fundamentals and strong consumer confidence, despite global challenges.

title: Meet INS Sudarshini: Indian Navy's sailing ambassador in US
content: India's sail training ship INS Sudarshini arrived in Baltimore on June 26, 2026, as part of the Lokayan 26 expedition. This visit, following participation in Sail250 Virginia, underscores India's maritime heritage and strong ties with the US Navy. The ship, a three-masted barque, will engage in maritime and community activities ahead of the US's 250th anniversary celebrations, showcasing its role as a floating ambassador.

title: Justin Gaethje calls out Conor McGregor for a fight after UFC 329
content: Justin Gaethje has made it clear that retirement is not on his mind just yet. The newly crowned UFC lightweight champion believes he still has a few fights left. Instead of focusing on defending his title, Gaethje is excited about the possibility of finally sharing the Octagon with Conor McGregor.

title: FIFA World Cup: Iran's locker room message after Egypt draw goes viral
content: Iran's football team garnered admiration for their sportsmanship, leaving a heartfelt message in the dressing room after their World Cup draw against Egypt. The note emphasized respect and fair play over victory. This follows a similar gesture after their match against Belgium. On the field, Egypt achieved a historic World Cup knockout stage qualification with the 1-1 draw.

title: Venezuela earthquakes: How India's armed forces have become a global first responder
content: India's armed forces have once again showcased their global humanitarian reach. Following devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, a 41-member rescue team and a field hospital were swiftly dispatched. This mission echoes past successful operations like those in Nepal, Yemen, and South Sudan, highlighting India's robust capability in rapid disaster response and civilian evacuation worldwide, extending far beyond national defense.

title: Oil Tanker Hit By "Unknown Projectile" In Hormuz As Iran, US Exchange Fire
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards announced on Saturday that they had attacked American positions in the Gulf after US strikes on Iran.

title: YSRCP Alleges Attack By TDP Supporters Over Amaravati Land Row
content: The incident triggered a political blame game between the ruling TDP and the opposition YSRCP over the ongoing land acquisition dispute in the capital region

title: PM Modi Meets World's Oldest Living Land Animal During Seychelles Visit
content: This visit is expected to consolidate cooperation between India and Seychelles in areas of maritime security, blue economy, climate resilience and capacity building.

title: Raids In 3 Kashmir Districts In Terror Funding Case, Digital Devices Seized
content: Acting on intel inputs, CIK sleuths on Saturday launched simultaneous search operations at three locations across the Valley as part of an ongoing investigation into terror-funding and other unlawful related activities.

title: Amit Shah Launches Bharat Taxi In Gujarat, Says Other Apps Exploit Drivers
content: While other apps exploit drivers, Shah said he can guarantee that the same will not happen in Bharat Taxi.

title: 'Easier To Kill Than Telling Family': A Shocking Confession In Pune Murder
content: Siya Goyal revealed that she chose to kill her fiance rather than calling off her wedding since she did not want to hurt her family's sentiments, sources said.

title: Germany To Decide On Mandatory Military Service Amid Soldier Shortage
content: Faced with a growing threat from Russia and an unpredictable United States, Berlin is looking to revamp its army and has committed to increase its troop strength to at least 260,000 soldiers -- up from the current 185,000 -- by 2035.

title: Venezuela Earthquake Crisis Tests Legitimacy Of Delcy Rodriguez Regime
content: The earthquakes that struck Venezuela on Wednesday have killed nearly 1,000 people and more than 3,300.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: New 4.9 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Venezuela
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Another Setback For MK Stalin's DMK, MDMK Quits Alliance After 9 Years
content: Vaiko's party justified its exit from the DMK-led alliance by alleging that "attempts were made to weaken the MDMK".

title: Small Plane Crashes Into Beijing's Tallest Building, Debris Falls From Sky
content: Videos and images also showed a portion of the building damaged, as well as a tail section of the plane and the broken window of a cab on the ground.

title: Opinion: Opinion | For The Great Passport Debate, A 'Common-Sense' Cure - By Shashi Tharoor
content: There is a gap between legal theory and the practical realities of a passport. But an easy fix can resolve this manufactured crisis.

title: Sagar Adani Calls For Faster Electrification To Boost Energy Security, Growth
content: Speaking at the inaugural Adani Green Energy Dialogue, Sagar Adani said that energy security, affordability and sustainability are among the defining challenges of our time.

title: Tukaram Mundhe: IAS Officer At Centre Of Maharashtra Food Regulator Row
content: Among Maharashtra's most recognisable IAS officers, Tukaram Haribhau Mundhe has built a reputation for being uncompromising and fiercely independent.

title: 'Trump, PM Modi Couldn't Be Closer': Top US Diplomat On Ties With India
content: The Trump administration holds PM Modi in high regard, and India is seen as the US's closest strategic partners, said Marco Rubio.

title: 13 Hotels, 1 Coaching Centre Sealed Amid Fire Safety Crackdown In Ahmedabad
content: In cases where safety protocols were completely ignored, fire authorities took action by sealing 13 hotels and one prominent coaching institute to prevent potential hazards.

title: CCTV Camera Covered, Cash Hidden In Toilet: Inside Story Of Ram Temple Theft
content: Fresh details have emerged on how the counting staff dug into the hundis without being noticed and made away with a fortune that belonged to Ram Lalla.

title: Lashkar Terrorists Attend Shoaib Akhtar's Brother's Funeral, Shows Video
content: Those who attended Shahid Akhtar's funeral included the leadership of the terror outfit Lashkar-e-Taiba's political wing.

title: 'When is PM Mann Ki Baat on this?' Priyank Kharge on Ram Mandir funds row
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide.
